name: source-driven-development
description: Verify framework / library API against official docs before writing code

positive_triggers.input: Use the new React Server Components API in this app
positive_triggers.reason: Unfamiliar / new API, must verify against docs
Major version upgrade, breaking changes likely

positive_triggers.input: Why does this useEffect fire twice in development?
positive_triggers.reason: Debugging requires knowing the documented behavior, not guessing

negative_triggers.input: Use Array.prototype.map to transform this list
Sort this list of numbers in ascending order
negative_triggers.reason: Pure business logic, no external API

negative_triggers.input: What does the existing code in this file do?
negative_triggers.reason: Reading existing code, not writing new API calls

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Use the new React Server Components API'
expected_behavior: Agent identifies library: react, current version in package.json
Agent queries official docs (react.dev) or context7 MCP
Agent extracts exact signature and behavior of the new API
Agent writes findings.md with: source URL, access date, exact version, key excerpt
Agent confirms docs version matches installed version
Agent cites source in code comment when using non-obvious API
Agent reads Next.js 15 migration guide from official docs
Agent extracts breaking changes (codemod, deprecated APIs)
Agent checks installed version: 'npm ls next'
Agent writes findings.md with breaking changes and migration steps
Agent identifies which existing code will break (based on docs, not memory)

notes: Behavioral evals are warning-level until promoted via upstream issue tracker.
